content.verdict: ION Adventure Hotel is the design hotel for couples who want their Iceland honeymoon to feel architecturally memorable. The property juts out of a lava field in Nesjavellir — 45 minutes from Reykjavík, on the doorstep of Þingvellir National Park — with a cantilevered concrete-and-glass form that feels like something from a Bond film. Rooms are compact but meticulously designed: blackout drapes, lava-gravel walls, recycled-timber headboards, and huge windows facing a landscape that can shift from green summer moss to white lunar snow in a week. The hotel's Northern Lights Bar is genuinely an attraction — a long glass wing with low lighting, designed for aurora spotting — and the outdoor geothermal pool, carved into the hillside, is one of the most photographed honeymoon moments in the country. Add a kitchen doing confident farm-to-table Icelandic cooking and you have a stylish, scenic base for exploring the south and west.
content.best_room: Northern Lights Room — floor-to-ceiling windows facing the lava plain and the aurora corridor, king bed, rainfall shower, and a reading nook oriented at the view.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival and first pool soak
itinerary_7_nights.description: Transfer from Keflavík (75 minutes), check-in, a welcome cocktail at the Northern Lights Bar, and an evening soak in the geothermal outdoor pool.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Þingvellir and Silfra fissure
itinerary_7_nights.description: Short drive to Þingvellir National Park, a guided snorkel or dive between the tectonic plates at Silfra, and lunch at a lakeside café.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Reykjavík culture day
itinerary_7_nights.description: Day in the capital — Harpa, Hallgrímskirkja, the FlyOver Iceland experience, and dinner at a Reykjavík tasting-menu restaurant before returning to ION.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Golden Circle
itinerary_7_nights.description: Full Golden Circle loop including Geysir and Gullfoss, a stop at Kerið crater, and a late-afternoon return for a sauna and aurora watch.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Snæfellsnes peninsula
itinerary_7_nights.description: Long day trip to the 'Iceland in miniature' peninsula — Kirkjufell mountain, Djúpalónssandur black pebble beach, and the Snæfellsjökull glacier — returning under star-pricked skies.

itinerary_7_nights.title: South Coast waterfalls
itinerary_7_nights.description: Seljalandsfoss, Skógafoss, and Reynisfjara black-sand beach, with an ice-cave or glacier-walk add-on in winter.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Spa and farewell
itinerary_7_nights.description: Slow morning, couples massage in the ION spa, a final soak in the outdoor pool, and a short transfer back to Keflavík.

caveats: The hotel is remote — there is no town or walkable dining nearby, so you will drive or use guided transfers for every meal outside the hotel.
Rooms are well designed but not huge; if you want a suite-scale honeymoon footprint, pre-select one of the corner or signature rooms.
The outdoor pool is heated geothermally but the walk to it is cold in winter — bring or borrow a robe and sandals.

faqs.question: Is this hotel good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: Yes — it's a design-led boutique with a geothermal pool, aurora-optimized bar, and strong restaurant in one of Iceland's most dramatic landscapes. A very good pick for style-conscious couples.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit?
faqs.answer: September to March for aurora, June to August for midnight-sun hiking and Silfra snorkeling. The surrounding national park is spectacular year-round.

faqs.question: What should we book in advance?
faqs.answer: A Northern Lights Room, a Silfra snorkel or dive slot, Golden Circle and Snæfellsnes excursions, and a dinner reservation at the hotel restaurant on peak nights.

faqs.question: Is it adults-only?
faqs.answer: No, ION is not adults-only, but it attracts a mostly adult, design-interested clientele and the mood is generally quiet and focused on nature.

faqs.question: What's the best room type for a honeymoon?
faqs.answer: The Northern Lights Room with its panoramic view is the signature honeymoon choice. The Junior Suite adds more floor space and a lounge area if the budget stretches.

faqs.question: How do we get there?
faqs.answer: Fly into Keflavík (KEF). The hotel is about 75 minutes away by road; book a private transfer or rent a car for flexibility on excursions.
